tasks.title: Add TSV loading support
tasks.prompt: Add a `load_tsv` function to `src/csvstats/loader.py` that loads tab-separated files and returns the same `list[dict[str, str]]` as `load_rows`. It should accept a `pathlib.Path`, use the csv module with `delimiter='\t'`, and log errors the same way `load_rows` does. Add a test for it in `tests/test_loader.py` using a small TSV fixture. Run `pytest -q` to confirm everything passes.

tasks.title: Use LoadError in loader.py
tasks.prompt: Refactor `src/csvstats/loader.py` so that when a file is not found, it raises `LoadError` from `csvstats.errors` instead of the raw `FileNotFoundError`. Import `LoadError` at the top. Update all existing tests in `tests/test_loader.py` to expect `LoadError` instead of `FileNotFoundError`. Run `pytest -q` to confirm everything passes.

tasks.title: Add --delimiter CLI flag
tasks.prompt: Add a `--delimiter` flag to the CLI in `src/csvstats/cli.py` that lets the user specify a custom column delimiter (default `,`). Pass it through to `csv.DictReader` in `load_rows`. Add a test in `tests/test_loader.py` that exercises a pipe-delimited file. Run `pytest -q` to confirm everything passes.

tasks.title: Add median to report
tasks.prompt: Add a `median` field to the report output for any numeric column. In `src/csvstats/report.py`, update `build_report` so that for each column whose values are all convertible to float, the summary includes the median. Add a test for it in `tests/test_report.py` using a small CSV with known numeric values. Run `pytest -q` to confirm everything passes.
